text.title: Writing Specifications: 6-Phase Workflow
text.summary: Brainstorm → Research → Clarify → Draft → Review → Save procedure for writing spec.md, with Socratic dialogue techniques and anti-patterns.

rule.statement: Start every spec session with the problem statement, never the solution; ask "what pain?" before "what feature?"
rule.rationale: Solution-first specs embed implementation assumptions into requirements. Problem-first specs leave design decisions to the design phase. When a spec says "add a button that exports to CSV," it has already made a UI and format decision that belongs in design.md.

list.item: Intent is source of truth — spec is the main artifact, not a reflection of a prior decision
Socratic dialogue — user formulates requirements through questions, not monologue
Incremental validation — show each section, get approval, then continue
Opening: "Tell me about the problem. Who suffers and how?"
Five Whys: for each answer, ask "Why?" — "Need export" → "Why?" → "Managers ask" → "Why?" → "No access" → Real problem: UX gap
Alternatives: for each idea, present A/B with pros/cons, ask "Which is closer?"
Challenge: "Is this needed for v1?", "What if we DON'T do this?", "Does this already exist in the codebase?"

rule.statement: Ask one question at a time in Socratic dialogue; wait for response before asking the next.
rule.rationale: Agents interpret "Socratic dialogue" as asking multiple questions in a batch then proceeding. Single-question pauses enforce genuine elicitation. Without this, agents hallucinate answers to unanswered questions.

list.item: Phase 2 (Research): grep codebase for similar implementations; share findings: "Found existing export in services.py. Does this affect the approach?"
Phase 3 (Clarify): user stories workshop — "As {role}, I want {goal}, so that {benefit}. How often? What happens if this fails?"; ask edge cases as questions, never assume
Problem Statement → show → validate: "Is this correct?"
User Stories with AC → show → validate: "Is this complete?"
Out of Scope → show → validate: "Agree with boundaries?" (capture while fresh)
Phase 5 (Review): verify problem is SMART, user stories follow INVEST, requirements are testable, out-of-scope is explicit; call faion-sdd-reviewer-agent (mode: spec)
Phase 6 (Save): new feature → .aidocs/features/backlog/{NN}-{feature}/spec.md; existing feature → update spec.md in existing folder

antipattern.description: Agent makes assumptions instead of asking questions when user says "I don't know".
antipattern.reason: "I don't know" is valid input that must be documented as uncertainty, not interpolated. Agents that fill uncertainty with plausible answers produce specs that look complete but contain fabricated requirements.

antipattern.description: Writing large blocks of spec without incremental validation.
antipattern.reason: Errors in section 1 propagate to sections 2-8. Show and validate each section before proceeding; fixing a requirement after five dependent sections are written requires rewriting all downstream sections.
